mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% as of 2023-04-15 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and sell equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2023-04-15 remains below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, causing equity valuations to compress and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following RSAFS declines exceeding 1% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rising above 105 combined with inflation-adjusted payroll growth signals robust supply-side capacity and productivity gains, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both INDPRO > 105 and PAYEMS growth > 0.2% MoM versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST declining below 1000K units signals contraction in residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1127K as of 2023-03-15 remains above trigger
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with tightening financial conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where HOUST falls below 1000K, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 250 combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 2% creates a negative real interest rate regime that incentivizes equity allocation over bonds, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both CPIAUCSL > 250 and FEDFUNDS < 2% versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment measured by PAYEMS declining month-over-month signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, causing equity valuations to compress and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances following PAYEMS monthly declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerating above 2.5% annualized combined with rising retail sales RSAFS signals robust aggregate demand and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 0.3% month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both GDPC1 growth > 2.5% and RSAFS growth > 0.3% MoM versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.045 over any 3-year rolling window.
